metadata.description: Preferred resources for Conflict Mapper. Check these sources first for updated information, articles, and analysis before searching other sites.
metadata.instructions: When researching any topic covered by these sources, check them FIRST before general web search. Add new resources via the 'Save to Preferred Resources' workflow.

sources.name: Institute for Science and International Security (ISIS)
sources.description: Premier source for nuclear facility analysis, satellite imagery assessment, and proliferation tracking. David Albright's team provides the most detailed post-strike damage assessments of Iranian nuclear sites.

sources.name: Arms Control Association
sources.description: Authoritative source on arms control agreements, nuclear policy, and weapons treaties. Maintains detailed facility status trackers for Iran.

sources.name: Arms Control Center
sources.description: Fact sheets and analysis on nuclear weapons programs, facilities, and arms control policy.

sources.name: Center for Nonproliferation Studies (CNS)
sources.description: Monterey Institute affiliate. Research on WMD nonproliferation, export controls, and disarmament.

sources.name: SIPRI — Stockholm International Peace Research Institute
sources.description: World's premier independent institute on conflict, armaments, arms control, and disarmament. Publisher of the SIPRI Yearbook — definitive compendium on military expenditure, arms transfers, and nuclear arsenals.

sources.name: Institute for the Study of War (ISW)
sources.description: Most widely cited daily source of open-source intelligence on active conflicts. Daily assessments of Russia-Ukraine and Iran war operations. Maps and operational assessments cited by governments and military analysts worldwide.

sources.name: The War Zone (TWZ)
sources.description: Detailed military technology and operations coverage. Excellent for weapons platform analysis, strike assessments, and operational details of ongoing conflicts.

sources.name: International Crisis Group (ICG)
sources.description: Gold standard for field-based conflict analysis and early warning. Watch List publications identify countries most at risk of conflict escalation.

sources.name: IISS — International Institute for Strategic Studies
sources.description: Publisher of The Military Balance — definitive reference on armed forces and defense budgets of 170+ countries. Organizes Shangri-La Dialogue.

sources.name: Brookings Institution
sources.description: One of the most cited policy research organizations. Covers foreign policy, economics, governance, and international security.

sources.name: Carnegie Endowment for International Peace
sources.description: Global network of policy research centers in US, Russia, China, Europe, Middle East, and India.

sources.name: Council on Foreign Relations (CFR)
sources.description: Most prestigious American foreign policy institution. Publishes Foreign Affairs journal and Global Conflict Tracker.

sources.name: RAND Corporation
sources.description: Most methodologically rigorous policy research organization. Defense, national security, AI/emerging tech.

sources.name: Center for Strategic and International Studies (CSIS)
sources.description: Bipartisan Washington think tank. Defense and National Security Center of Excellence. Influential on China, Indo-Pacific, defense modernization.

sources.name: JINSA — Jewish Institute for National Security of America
sources.description: US-Israel security relationship, Middle East defense policy, Iran threat assessment.

sources.name: Stimson Center
sources.description: Nonpartisan policy research center on international peace and security. Notable Iran post-conflict scenario analysis.

sources.name: Eurasia Group
sources.description: World's leading geopolitical risk advisory. Annual Top Risks report by Ian Bremmer.

sources.name: Chatham House
sources.description: One of world's most respected international affairs institutes. Named for the Chatham House Rule.

sources.name: Royal United Services Institute (RUSI)
sources.description: World's oldest defense and security think tank (1831). Declared 'undesirable' in Russia — testament to its Ukraine analysis quality.

sources.name: Atlantic Council
sources.description: Foremost US institution on transatlantic security and NATO affairs. Scowcroft Center for Strategy and Security.

sources.name: Belfer Center — Harvard Kennedy School
sources.description: Harvard's premier national security research institution. Graham Allison's Avoiding Great Power War Project.

sources.name: Lowy Institute
sources.description: Australia's leading international policy think tank. Asia-Pacific focus. Annual Lowy Institute Poll.

sources.name: MERICS — Mercator Institute for China Studies
sources.description: Largest European research institute on contemporary China. Sanctioned by Beijing for independence of research.

sources.name: Observer Research Foundation (ORF)
sources.description: India's most prominent international think tank. Hosts Raisina Dialogue.

sources.name: SWP — German Institute for International Security Affairs
sources.description: Advises German Bundestag, Federal Government, EU, NATO, and UN.

sources.name: CIA — Iran Nuclear Sites (Abbottabad Compound)
sources.description: CIA reference document on Iranian nuclear site locations. Found in Abbottabad compound raid materials.

sources.name: IAEA — International Atomic Energy Agency
sources.description: Official UN nuclear watchdog. Safeguards inspections, verification reports, Board of Governors decisions.
